message:
fix(core): replace unreachable!() with explicit error in policy inject, close SSH config override bead, apply rustfmt

policy.rs — replace unreachable!() with proper error return:
- The inject() match arm for non-injection ActionKind variants previously
  called unreachable!(), which would panic at runtime if a new ActionKind
  was added without updating the match. Replace with an explicit
  InjectionResult::Error return that includes the unexpected action kind
  in the error message, making the failure diagnosable instead of a bare
  panic with no context.

rustfmt import sorting (toml_config.rs, mux/ssh.rs, ssh/sessioninner.rs):
- Apply Rust 2024 edition import ordering (alphabetical within groups)
  across three files. No behavioral change.

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- Close ft-4iowo (SSH config path override handling) — the fix was
  landed in 69574aa9 (tuning config round 2 / SSH config file override).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3055,7 +3055,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-4df","title":"[Human command] `wa backup export` (database + config archive with checksum)","description":"# Task: wa backup export

## Goal
Create portable backup archives that users can store, copy, and restore from.

## Command Design
```bash
# Basic export
$ wa backup export
Backup saved: ~/.local/share/wa/backups/wa_backup_2026-01-18_143015.wa

# Custom output
$ wa backup export --output /path/to/backup.wa

# With compression
$ wa backup export --compress

# Include specific workspaces only
$ wa backup export --workspace /project/a --workspace /project/b

# Exclude large transcript data (config/events only)
$ wa backup export --metadata-only
```

## Output Format
Archive structure (tar + optional zstd):
```
backup.wa/
├── manifest.json      # Version, timestamp, checksums
├── schema_version     # Plain text: "3"
├── database.sql       # SQLite dump
├── config.toml        # Effective configuration
├── patterns/          # Custom pattern packs
│   └── custom.toml
└── checksums.sha256   # Per-file checksums
```

manifest.json:
```json
{
  "wa_version": "0.1.0",
  "schema_version": 3,
  "created_at": "2026-01-18T14:30:15Z",
  "workspaces": ["/project/a", "/project/b"],
  "stats": {
    "segments": 12345,
    "events": 567,
    "panes": 8
  },
  "checksum": "sha256:abc123..."
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Use SQLite `.dump` for portability
- Acquire read lock during export (snapshot isolation)
- Stream large exports to avoid memory issues
- Verify checksum immediately after write

## Testing
- Unit tests: archive format validation, manifest generation
- Integration: export with active watcher, verify no corruption
- E2E: export + import round-trip preserves all data

## Acceptance Criteria
- Export creates valid, portable archive
- Checksum included and verified on write
- Progress shown for large databases
- Metadata-only mode for quick config backup

 {"id":"ft-4enj","title":"Scheduler + alert execution for saved searches","description":"## What
Add a background scheduler to execute saved searches and emit alerts.

## Why
Scheduled alerts turn wa into a proactive monitor rather than a pull-only tool.

## How
- Integrate with watcher runtime loop or dedicated task
- Enforce max frequency + backoff
- Emit notification events with redacted snippets + counts

## Risks
- Alert storm risk; must rate-limit and include dedupe window

## Success Criteria
- Scheduled searches run on interval
- Alerts include query name, scope, and match count","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:42.921020652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:56.969662Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:36:56.969669Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4f9ef","title":"[audit][T5.1] Move policy rate limit window and audit retention to TuningConfig","description":"# Move policy rate limit window and audit retention to TuningConfig

## Constants
- policy.rs ~1966: RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW = 60s -> tuning.policy.rate_limit_window_secs
- recorder_audit.rs 44: DEFAULT_AUDIT_RETENTION_DAYS = 90 -> tuning.audit.retention_days
- recorder_audit.rs 50: DEFAULT_APPROVAL_TTL_SECONDS = 900 -> tuning.audit.approval_ttl_secs
- recorder_audit.rs 47: DEFAULT_MAX_RAW_QUERY_ROWS = 100 -> tuning.audit.max_raw_query_rows
- replay_artifact_registry.rs 35: DEFAULT_RETENTION_DAYS = 30 -> tuning.audit.artifact_retention_days
- replay_shadow_rollout.rs 20: DEFAULT_SHADOW_DAYS = 14 -> tuning.audit.shadow_rollout_days

## Context
RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW controls the sliding window for send_text rate limiting. At 60s, an operator who sets max_per_second=2 gets exactly 2 sends/sec measured over 60s. Some SLOs need 30s windows (tighter control) or 300s (smoother averaging).

Audit retention (90 days default) is the biggest compliance lever. GDPR shops may need 30 days; SOC2 may need 365. Making this configurable avoids recompilation for compliance changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Constants replaced with config reads
2. Validation: retention >= 1 day, TTL >= 60s, window >= 10s
3. Defaults match current values","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeMill","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:40.968236Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:56:20.807298Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","config","policy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-4f9ef","depends_on_id":"ft-fled9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:59.431043Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2503,"issue_id":"ft-4f9ef","author":"BronzeMill","text":"Claimed by BronzeMill. -{"id":"ft-4iowo","title":"[audit][T9.6] Fix SSH config path override handling","description":"# Fix SSH config path override handling

## Problem
frankenterm/ssh/src/sessioninner.rs line 194:
  sess.options_parse_config(None)?; // FIXME: overridden config path?

The SSH session always parses the default SSH config (~/.ssh/config) and ignores any user-specified config path override. This means operators who use non-standard SSH config locations cannot configure SSH behavior for ft.

## Fix
Thread the config path override from ft.toml [ssh] section through to the libssh session initialization.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Custom SSH config path is respected
2. FIXME comment removed
3. Default behavior (None = ~/.ssh/config) preserved when no override specified","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:24.181283Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T19:49:10.761250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","ssh","vendored"]}

 {"id":"ft-4ja5","title":"Progressive disclosure: default/verbose/debug output levels","description":"# Progressive disclosure (verbosity)

## Goal
Show essential information by default, with increasing detail available on request, without sacrificing scriptability.

## Key design rule
- `-v/-vv` controls **how much** information we include.
- `--format {auto|plain|json}` controls **how** we render it.

This keeps CLI behavior consistent and avoids a proliferation of ad-hoc `--json` flags.

## Verbosity levels
- Default (no flag): Essential info only
- `-v`: Additional context
- `-vv`: Debug details

## JSON mode
- Use `--format json` for machine-readable output.
- JSON output must be schema-stable and compatible with non-TTY usage.

## Example: `wa status`
```bash
# Default
$ wa status
Panes: 4 observed, 1 ignored
Events: 2 unhandled

# Verbose
$ wa status -v
Panes:
  1: codex [PromptActive] /home/user/project (last: 2m ago)
  3: claude [CommandRunning] /home/user/other (last: 5s ago)
  5: local [Ignored] (exclude rule: title=htop)
  7: gemini [PromptActive] /home/user/gemini (last: 30s ago)

Events (unhandled):
  - codex.usage_limit_reached (Pane 1, 2m ago)
  - session.compaction (Pane 3, 5m ago)

# Debug
$ wa status -vv
[as above, plus:]
Health:
  Queue depth: 0
  Ingest lag: 12ms
  DB size: 1.2GB
  Circuit breakers: all closed

# Machine
$ wa status --format json | jq .
```

## Implementation sketch
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
struct Cli {
    #[arg(short, long, action = ArgAction::Count)]
    verbose: u8,
}

fn format_status(status: &Status, verbosity: u8) -> String {
    match verbosity {
        0 => format_status_brief(status),
        1 => format_status_verbose(status),
        _ => format_status_debug(status),
    }
}
```

## Consistency rules
- All human commands support `-v` and `-vv`.
- Default shows what the user needs to know.
- Verbose adds context for understanding.
- Debug adds internal state for troubleshooting.
- `--format json` output must not contain ANSI escapes and must be schema-stable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands support `-v/-vv`.
- [ ] Default output is clean and minimal.
- [ ] Verbose adds useful context.
- [ ] Debug shows internal state.
- [ ] Consistent pattern across all commands.

## Testing
- Unit tests for verbosity routing (brief/verbose/debug paths).
- Contract tests:
  - `--format json` schema validation
  - no ANSI escapes in non-TTY/plain/json
- Help/output snapshot tests where appropriate (normalized).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:23.666085891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.219265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T04:53:54.213378616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-4jxq","title":"CI e2e (extended): run approval allow-once scenario + keep artifacts","description":"# Task: CI extended e2e job

## Goal
Run the phase-4 "approval allow-once" E2E scenario in CI once the feature set is available.

This job specifically validates:
- RequireApproval UX payload
- wa approve command
- approval storage
- audited outcomes + redaction

## Behavior
- Execute:
  - ./scripts/e2e_test.sh --case approval-allow-once --verbose
- Always upload artifacts when failing.

## Registry
- Not a case. CI job extends the registry run with additional approval/allow-once scenarios.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A CI failure includes enough artifacts to diagnose whether the bug is in:
  - policy decision
  - approval granting
  - retry behavior
  - audit/export


## Testing
- CI self-check:
  - Ensure the job logs include the artifacts upload location.
  - Consider a “known fail” dry run to verify artifact upload and summary formatting.

 {"id":"ft-4ohrd","title":"[audit][T8.3] Make CLICK_INTERVAL user-configurable for accessibility","description":"# Make CLICK_INTERVAL user-configurable for accessibility

## Problem
frankenterm/term/src/input.rs line 68:
  CLICK_INTERVAL = Duration::from_millis(500)

This controls the maximum time between clicks for double-click detection. 500ms is the industry standard default but is NOT suitable for all users:
- Users with motor impairments may need 1000-2000ms
- Power users with fast reflexes may prefer 300ms
- Accessibility guidelines (WCAG, ADA) recommend this be configurable

## Fix
1. Expose click_interval_ms in the vendored config system
2. Default to 500ms
3. Document in accessibility section

## Why priority 2 (not 3 like other vendored)
Accessibility is a higher-priority concern than other vendored constants. This is a user-facing interaction parameter that directly affects usability for people with disabilities.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. CLICK_INTERVAL reads from config
2. Default is 500ms
3. Documented as accessibility-relevant setting","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:32.733301Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:33:51.322594Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["accessibility","audit","config","vendored"]}
